content.full_text:
August 3, 1976

Ms. Indira G. Clark  
Stanislaus Safe Energy Committee  
Post Office Box 2124  
Modesto, California 95354  

Dear Ms. Clark:

Thank you for your letter of July 31, requesting information concerning your obligations to file campaign statements under the Political Reform Act.

The law states that campaign statements should be filed prior to the election and once after the election is held. Your post-election campaign statement, form 420, should be filed on August 12, covering the period from May 25, 1976 through August 5, 1976. You will be required to report all activity, contributions received and expenditures made, during that period.

After this post-election statement has been filed, if your committee does not anticipate receiving any additional contributions or making any expenditures, no further reporting is required. If you have registered with the Secretary of State's office and received an identification number, I would suggest you write to that office and explain the current status of the committee.

This post-election campaign statement should be filed with the same filing officers who received your previous statements.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK:bw  
Enclosures  
bcc: Jeanne P.

July 31, 1976

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sirs:

We understand that there is an August deadline for filing for the June Primary; a follow up of the Form 420 which we filed last month. Will you please send the necessary forms and/or instructions so we can comply.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Indira G. Clark
Stanislaus Safe Energy Committee
P.O. Box 2124
631 15th Street
Modesto, California 95354

sections.question_synthetic: The requester is asking for clarification on the August filing deadline for a post-primary campaign statement (Form 420) and requesting the necessary forms and instructions to comply.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the post-election Form 420 must be filed by August 12, 1976, covering May 25 through August 5, 1976, reporting all contributions and expenditures during that period. After filing this statement, no further reporting is required if the committee does not anticipate additional contributions or expenditures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (dated July 31, 1976) and the FPPC response (dated August 3, 1976). Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Minor formatting: incoming letter appears after the response letter in the document.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the Stanislaus Safe Energy Committee's inquiry regarding their August filing deadline by confirming the Form 420 post-election campaign statement must be filed by August 12, 1976, and outlining the reporting requirements and subsequent obligations.
